attributes.action: Introduce Performance Related Pay for Councillors & Local Government Staff
attributes.additional_details:
At a time when residents are facing increasing council tax bills, service reductions and financial pressures, there must be greater transparency and accountability regarding how public money is spent on leadership roles within local government.

We Believe:

Publicly funded leadership positions should be subject to clear performance expectations in the same way many other professions are.

The performance measure should also be put out for public consultation. So we can help shape the process.
attributes.background: We call on the UK Government and local authorities to introduce stronger accountability measures by linking councillor allowances and senior local government pay progression to measurable performance, attendance and public service delivery.

rejection.details:
We can't accept your petition because the UK Government and Parliament aren't responsible for the issue you raise. The decision to introduce performance related pay is for individual local councils, not the UK Government or Parliament.

You could raise your issue with a local councillor who represents you. You can use this page to find out who your local councillors are, and how to contact them: https://www.gov.uk/find-your-local-councillors

You could start a new petition about something the UK Government and Parliament are responsible for. For example, you could start a new petition asking the UK Government and Parliament to require all councils by law to introduce performance related pay, if that's something you'd like to happen.
